evidence_excerpt: After CT confirmed bilateral talocalcaneal coalitions, she underwent resection; at 1-year follow-up her range of motion and gait had markedly improved and she remained pain-free.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: She underwent resection of bilateral talocalcaneal coalitions after CT diagnosis.
At 1 year follow-up, her range of motion and gait had markedly improved.
At 1 year follow-up, she remains pain-free.

notes_on_uncertainty: Index time is approximated as the CT-confirmed diagnosis of talocalcaneal coalitions prior to surgical treatment. Exact timing of surgery relative to diagnosis is not specified, but appears soon after CT. Long-term beyond 1 year is not described; residual limitations are not explicitly documented, so functional status was conservatively classified as mild and overall outcome as partial_recovery.
